features.summary: Raytheon Technologies reported Q2 2022 results with 4% organic sales growth to $16.3 billion and adjusted EPS of $1.16, up 13% year-over-year. Commercial aerospace recovery continued with aftermarket sales up 26%, while defense businesses faced significant supply chain and labor constraints. The company maintained full-year guidance of $67.75-68.75 billion in sales and $4.60-4.80 adjusted EPS, though expects to be toward the midpoint due to defense headwinds partially offset by stronger commercial performance.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued commercial aerospace recovery with international travel reaching 75-80% of 2019 levels by year-end. Defense outlook remains positive with growing global budgets and strong bookings, though execution challenges persist. Supply chain constraints are expected to continue through 2022, with kit fill rates targeted to improve from 50% to 80% by year-end. The company remains confident in achieving 2025 targets despite near-term operational challenges.
features.risks: Primary risks include persistent supply chain disruptions affecting material availability, severe labor shortages particularly for skilled engineers with security clearances, and elevated inflation pressures. Defense businesses face execution challenges with productivity impacts from low kit fill rates. Geopolitical tensions with China could affect supply chain access, while the R&D tax capitalization legislation poses a $2 billion cash flow risk if not deferred.
features.key_drivers: Commercial aftermarket strength drives growth with Collins up 25% and Pratt up 26% in aftermarket sales. Defense bookings remain robust with 1.35 book-to-bill ratio and $65 billion backlog. Key program awards include $4 billion F135 engine contract and various missile system contracts. Supply chain recovery and labor availability improvements are critical for defense business performance in the second half.
